query: How does v-bind connect reactive state to DOM attributes?

competitors.label: Template Syntax
competitors.selectionReason: This is the canonical Vue guide for template compilation, attribute bindings, directives, and the render-time dependency reads that power v-bind.
theirStrengths: The official guide is authoritative on how template bindings are compiled and how reactive state drives DOM updates.
theirGaps: It is less explicit about the attribute-versus-property patching distinction and the interview-style patch-flag mental model.

realWorldUseCases: When those reactive values change, Vue re-runs the component render and then patches only the changed attributes/DOM props on the affected elements.
followUpConfusion: Vue’s patcher decides whether a binding should be applied as a DOM property

realWorldUseCases: The docs explain dynamic binding well, but our page is more explicit about how reactive reads during render lead to targeted DOM/property patching later.
actionableExamples: The official examples remain strong on binding syntax, so the example criterion stays tied.
followUpConfusion: Our answer is clearer on the likely follow-up around boolean attrs, class/style special handling, and why value/checked behave like DOM properties.

actionableExamples: Add one tiny input example later that contrasts :value with a plain attribute update.

competitors.winReason: The official docs own the syntax and compilation basics, but our page is stronger because it turns v-bind into a concrete render-read plus patch-write mental model.

competitors.label: Vue Interview Questions
competitors.selectionReason: GreatFrontEnd's Vue interview bank is directly relevant because template bindings, directives, and reactive DOM updates are core Vue interview fundamentals.
theirStrengths: It is a strong interview-prep hub for Vue binding and template questions.
theirGaps: The landing page is not dedicated to the under-the-hood v-bind update path.

realWorldUseCases: template compilation into a render function, dependency tracking during render, scheduled component updates
followUpConfusion: The runtime uses patch flags / dynamic props so it can update only what’s dynamic.

realWorldUseCases: The bank is relevant at the topic level, but it is less focused on the precise render-to-patch flow of a v-bind update.
actionableExamples: GreatFrontEnd remains competitive on example-driven practice, so the example criterion stays tied.
followUpConfusion: The category page is less explicit about why v-bind is not a special attribute watcher and how Vue decides property versus attribute writes.

actionableExamples: Keep the VNode-props explanation short so the answer stays interview-sized instead of becoming a renderer deep dive.

competitors.winReason: GreatFrontEnd is useful as a Vue fundamentals hub, but our page is stronger because it explains the exact runtime path from reactive read to patched attribute or property.

competitors.label: Vue.js Directives: A Beginner's Guide
competitors.selectionReason: Vue School has a directive-focused article that provides a strong tutorial baseline for how v-bind participates in reactive template behavior.
theirStrengths: It is a direct directive-focused tutorial and a good baseline for binding syntax and reactive DOM updates.
theirGaps: It is less explicit about patch flags, property-versus-attribute rules, and form-control edge cases.

realWorldUseCases: mapping them to DOM properties or HTML attributes (with special handling for class/style/booleans).
followUpConfusion: The exact output varies, but conceptually :src/:disabled/:class become a props object on the VNode.

realWorldUseCases: The tutorial explains directive usage well, but our page is more specific about the exact DOM/property patching consequences of v-bind.
actionableExamples: Its syntax examples are useful, so the example criterion stays tied.
followUpConfusion: Our answer is stronger on the interview follow-up around VNode props and why some bindings are applied as properties rather than HTML attributes.
